fallback.title: System timeline event
fallback.detail: Aming Claw recorded a public timeline event for this task; the narrative and chips summarize the safe fields before raw evidence.

templates.title: Timeline append authorized
templates.detail: The route token gate checked whether a protected timeline append could proceed and produced public authorization evidence.

narrative.actor: Route token gate evaluated a protected task-timeline append.
narrative.information: It produced gate evidence about the allowed action, event kind, status, and route identity hashes.
narrative.context: The gate shows whether the append is tied to approved route context without displaying route tokens.
narrative.purpose: This protects close-gate lanes from unscoped timeline evidence.
narrative.outcome: The event records whether the timeline append was accepted, blocked, or needs more route evidence.

templates.title: Route service completed
templates.detail: The route service completed a scoped action check for the next observer or worker step and recorded the public service, action, decision, route identity hashes, and evidence implications.

narrative.actor: Route service completed a scoped governance action check for the observer or worker lane.
narrative.information: It recorded the public service id, requested action, decision, source event, and route identity hashes when present.
narrative.context: The visible context is limited to route ids, hashes, action labels, request ids, and source event names; private route context bodies are not displayed.
narrative.purpose: This tells a developer which action was completed, which actor can use the result, and whether the next governed step can proceed.
narrative.outcome: Allowed actions can continue under this route context; blocked or incomplete actions still need the missing evidence named by the close-gate banner.

templates.title: Worker startup recorded
templates.detail: A bounded manual-fix worker started inside its assigned branch/worktree lane with public fence evidence.

narrative.actor: Bounded worker started the assigned manual-fix lane.
narrative.information: It produced startup evidence for the worker identity, branch/worktree, fence, and owned files when present.
narrative.context: The startup record identifies the bounded task lineage and target commit; raw request text and route tokens are not displayed.
narrative.purpose: This proves implementation work is happening inside the dispatched worker boundary.
narrative.outcome: The worker lane moved from dispatched to started, which is required before review or close can trust worker evidence.

templates.title: Prompt context requested
templates.detail: The route service prepared public task scope for the next observer or bounded worker: target files, acceptance criteria, required evidence, and route identity hashes; private prompt and route bodies stay hidden.

narrative.actor: Route service requested or delivered bounded task context for the next observer or worker lane.
narrative.information: It obtained public task scope such as target files, acceptance criteria, required evidence, route id, prompt contract id, and visible manifest hash when present.
narrative.context: The context tells the receiving actor what can be done, which files or criteria are in scope, and what evidence the close gate still expects; private prompt text and route context bodies are hidden.
narrative.purpose: This gives the next actor enough public-safe scope to dispatch, implement, or verify without exposing private route material.
narrative.outcome: The next implication is a protected dispatch or action request; until implementation, verification, and close-ready events exist, the close-gate blocker remains visible.

templates.title: Route action requested
templates.detail: A route action check recorded who requested the governed action, which action was authorized or blocked, what route evidence changed, and what evidence must appear before close.

narrative.actor: Route service checked a scoped governance action for the requesting actor.
narrative.information: It produced action-precheck evidence for the requested action, allowed action, stage, decision, route hash, and backlog/task scope when present.
narrative.context: The action request carries public route identity and allowed-action metadata; route tokens and private route bodies are not displayed.
narrative.purpose: This explains whether the next mutation, dispatch, or evidence append is allowed to proceed and which public route evidence supports it.
narrative.outcome: Allowed actions can continue under the route context; blocked actions need the missing route, worker, implementation, verification, or close-ready evidence before the task can close.

templates.title: Worker read receipt
templates.detail: Bounded worker received task context containing target files, acceptance criteria, allowed/blocked actions, route identity hashes, and required evidence; private prompt text is hidden.

narrative.actor: Bounded worker acknowledged the assigned task contract.
narrative.information: It recorded that bounded context was received and that forbidden actions and stop state were understood.
narrative.context: Bounded worker received task context containing target files, acceptance criteria, allowed/blocked actions, route identity hashes, and required evidence; private prompt text is hidden.
narrative.purpose: This proves the worker understood the implementation fence before producing evidence.
narrative.outcome: The worker lane recorded context consumption, allowing startup, implementation, or review gates to reason about the lane.

templates.title: Backlog close gate checked
templates.detail: The route token gate checked whether backlog close could proceed with the recorded public evidence.

narrative.actor: Route token gate evaluated a backlog close request.
narrative.information: It produced close-gate evidence such as can-close status, missing event kinds, and route identity fields when present.
narrative.context: The gate reads public timeline evidence and protected lane state; route tokens and private route bodies are not displayed.
narrative.purpose: This explains why the backlog can close or what evidence is still missing.
narrative.outcome: The close state changed to ready, blocked, or still waiting for required evidence.

templates.title: Backlog upsert authorized
templates.detail: The route token gate checked whether a public backlog upsert was authorized.

narrative.actor: Route token gate evaluated a backlog upsert request.
narrative.information: It produced upsert authorization evidence for the backlog id, route id, operation, and target files when present.
narrative.context: The visible context is the public route/upsert envelope; route tokens and private route bodies are not displayed.
narrative.purpose: This protects backlog mutations from unscoped updates.
narrative.outcome: The backlog upsert was authorized, blocked, or recorded for follow-up.

templates.title: Route token gate checked
templates.detail: The route token gate checked a protected governance action and recorded public gate evidence.

narrative.actor: Route token gate evaluated a protected action.
narrative.information: It produced public gate status, action, route identity hash, and missing-evidence fields when present.
narrative.context: The gate evidence explains route authorization without displaying route tokens or private route bodies.
narrative.purpose: This keeps protected timeline, backlog, and close actions tied to approved route context.
narrative.outcome: The protected action was accepted, blocked, or left waiting for route evidence.

templates.title: Route evidence blocked
templates.detail: A route or close-gate evidence attempt was blocked or recorded as waiver-only, with the missing protected evidence and next action kept public.

narrative.actor: Route gate or observer recorded a route evidence blocker.
narrative.information: It identified which protected event kinds, worker evidence, or route-consumption proof was missing.
narrative.context: This is diagnostic or waiver-only evidence; it does not replace implementation, verification, startup, dispatch, or close-ready evidence.
narrative.purpose: This prevents a backlog from closing just because raw repair details exist when protected route evidence is still missing.
narrative.outcome: The next implication is to add the missing bounded worker route-context or close-gate evidence before attempting close again.

templates.title: Worker dispatch recorded
templates.detail: The observer dispatched a bounded worker with target files, acceptance criteria, route identity hashes, and required evidence.

narrative.actor: Observer dispatched a bounded implementation worker.
narrative.information: It produced dispatch evidence for worker identity, file fence, branch/worktree, route identity, and graph trace ids when present.
narrative.context: Dispatch context contains target files, acceptance criteria, allowed/blocked actions, route identity hashes, and required evidence; private prompt text is hidden.
narrative.purpose: This starts implementation while preserving the observer-owned merge, close, and reconcile boundaries.
narrative.outcome: The task gained a bounded worker lane that can produce implementation evidence for review.

templates.title: Implementation evidence
templates.detail: Implementation work was recorded with public code, node, file, or test evidence.

narrative.information: It recorded changed files, graph traces, node ids, tests, or commit evidence when present.
narrative.context: The evidence describes the bounded code change without exposing private request bodies or host secrets.
narrative.purpose: This is the worker-produced proof that the requested implementation happened inside the file fence.
narrative.outcome: The implementation lane moved toward review, verification, or a blocked follow-up depending on status.

templates.title: Verification evidence
templates.detail: Verification was recorded with public test, QA, browser, or gate evidence.

narrative.actor: Verification lane checked the recorded work.
narrative.information: It produced public test results, QA status, warnings, screenshots, or gate checks when present.
narrative.context: The visible evidence is limited to public commands, pass/fail state, artifacts, and sanitized verification details.
narrative.purpose: This tells the observer whether implementation evidence is trustworthy enough for review or close.
narrative.outcome: The task state moved to passed, failed, blocked, or waiting based on verification status.

templates.title: Independent verification completed
templates.detail: An independent verification lane completed and recorded public QA evidence.

narrative.actor: Independent verification lane reviewed the worker evidence.
narrative.information: It produced public QA evidence, test results, graph traces, or changed-file checks when present.
narrative.context: The verifier uses public task evidence and sanitized artifacts, not private prompt or route-token bodies.
narrative.purpose: This separates worker output from verification so the observer can trust the handoff.
narrative.outcome: The verification lane recorded whether the candidate is ready for merge/close review.

templates.title: Observer close-ready evidence
templates.detail: The observer recorded public close-ready evidence for final review.

narrative.information: It produced a public review summary, commit/test evidence, and close-gate status when present.
narrative.context: Close-ready evidence summarizes implementation, verification, and risk without exposing private request text.
narrative.purpose: This is the explicit signal that the backlog row can enter close review.
narrative.outcome: The close gate can pass when this evidence and all required protected lanes are present.

templates.title: Manual fix close-ready evidence
templates.detail: Manual-fix close-ready evidence was recorded for the backlog row.

narrative.information: It produced public precommit, verification, risk, test, and changed-file evidence when present.
narrative.context: The evidence is scoped to the manual-fix backlog row and sanitized before display.
narrative.purpose: This gives the observer a compact close packet for review.
narrative.outcome: The task moves to close-ready when manual-fix evidence satisfies the required lanes.

templates.title: Close-ready evidence
templates.detail: Close-ready evidence was recorded for this task timeline.

narrative.information: It recorded public close status, commit, tests, changed files, and remaining missing evidence when present.
narrative.context: Close-ready context ties implementation and verification evidence to the backlog row without exposing private request bodies.
narrative.purpose: This is the final timeline signal needed before the close gate can pass.
narrative.outcome: The task becomes ready to close when close-ready evidence and required verification lanes are present.
